name: Axiom of Specification
guide_hints: # Level 5: The **axiom of specification** or **axiom of restricted comprehension** states that given a set A and a formula ő®(x), you can construct a set B which contains exactly those elements x of A which satisfy the formula ő®(x).
So basically it can be used to prove formulas of the form `‚ąÉB ‚ąÄx x‚ąąB ‚áĒ (x‚ąąA ‚ąß ő®(x))`. I hope you are able to comprehend this formal statement.
We will define a long term to define the **Set Builder Form** which of the type {<variable> ‚ąą <term> | <formula>}
